title: Print a planet enum
prompt_md:
## Goal
Overload `operator<<` for a `Planet` enum so it prints the planet's name.

## Input
None (hardcoded values).

## Output
Exact lines:
```
Mercury
Venus
Earth
Mars
```
(trailing newline after `Mars`)

## Requirements
1. Define an unscoped enum `Planet` with values `mercury`, `venus`, `earth`, `mars` (in that order).
2. Overload `std::ostream& operator<<(std::ostream& out, Planet p)` using a `switch` statement.
3. Each case must print the capitalised name (e.g. `Mercury`).
4. The function must return `out`.
5. `main` must print all four planets, each on its own line, using `std::cout << planet << '\n'`.

title: Fix the broken direction printer
prompt_md:
## Goal
Fix the single bug in the program below so it correctly reads a direction and prints it back.

## Input
One word on stdin: `north`, `south`, `east`, or `west`.

## Output
The same word, capitalised:
```
North
```
(trailing newline)

## Requirements
1. The program reads a direction word from stdin into a `Direction` variable using `operator>>`.
2. It then prints the direction name (capitalised) via `operator<<`.
3. The code already contains **one bug** — find and fix it. Do not change anything else.

## Notes
- The bug is in the parameter list of one of the operator overloads.
- Any unrecognised word should map to `west`.
